analysis.summary: The technician performed a configuration review within Cisco UCS Manager. After an initial failure to log into the KVM viewer, they successfully logged into UCS Manager to inspect the network settings of 'Service Profile Server-5', specifically reviewing its vNIC configurations, including MTU and associated VLANs.
actions_taken: Attempted to access a KVM viewer via `172.16.9.20`, encountering repeated authentication errors.
Navigated to the Cisco UCS Manager login page at `172.16.9.20`.
Logged into Cisco UCS Manager using 'admin' credentials.
Navigated to the 'Servers' section and selected 'Service Profile Server-5'.
Reviewed the physical properties of the associated server `sys/chassis-1/blade-5` (Cisco UCS B200 M4).
Navigated to the 'Network' tab for 'Service Profile Server-5'.
Inspected vNIC settings including MAC address assignment, Fabric ID (Fabric B), MTU (9000), associated VLANs (AI-VLAN10, AI-VLAN100, etc.), Adapter Policy (VMware), and QoS Policy (40GbpsLmiPolic).
issues_observed: "Login Error: Authentication failed" when attempting to access the KVM Viewer at `https://172.16.9.20/app/4_0_4/kvm.html`.
notable_events: Initial attempts to access the KVM Viewer resulted in repeated authentication failures, leading the technician to switch to the main UCS Manager interface.
A text file containing network interface configurations (VLANs, HSRP, IP addresses) was open at the beginning of the session, likely as reference.

stuck_details: The technician encountered a "Login Error: Authentication failed" pop-up when trying to access the KVM Viewer at 0:10. After clicking OK, the error reappeared at 0:17, indicating a repeated failure to access the KVM viewer, lasting approximately 8 seconds before the technician navigated to the main UCS Manager login page.
tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician initially faced a login issue with the KVM viewer but quickly adapted by navigating to the main UCS Manager. Once logged in, the navigation and review of the server's network configuration were methodical and thorough.
coaching_flags: Review proper access methods or credentials for the KVM viewer before initiating the connection to avoid repeated login errors.
